description_long: This is a tall, slender stone obelisk with a pointed tip, mounted on a multi-tiered rectangular base. The front face features a carved decorative motif resembling a floral or symbolic relief, adding a touch of ornamentation to the otherwise plain surfaces. The stone appears weathered, with subtle discoloration and surface texture variations, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The base consists of several stacked rectangular layers, each slightly smaller than the one below, providing stability and a sense of monumentality. The obelisk is constructed from a solid, light-gray stone, likely granite or marble, and stands as a prominent vertical marker suitable for parks, plazas, or memorial sites. No visible inscriptions are present, and the surrounding area is clear of additional features.
description: A tall, weathered stone obelisk with a pointed tip and a multi-tiered rectangular base, featuring a carved relief on the front. The structure is solid, light-gray, and visually imposing.
description_view_0: Front view showing the carved relief motif on the upper section and the layered rectangular base.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the plain, unadorned surface of the obelisk and the stepped base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a smooth, undecorated face and the full height of the obelisk.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with visible weathering and the same stepped base structure.
attribute_ornamentation: A carved relief motif is present on the front face of the obelisk, located in the upper section below the tip. The design appears floral or symbolic.
attribute_base_design: The base is multi-tiered and rectangular, composed of several stacked stone layers, each slightly smaller than the one below. The design is simple and robust, with no additional decorative features.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is sharply pointed, forming a classic obelisk pyramidion with a smooth finish and no additional decoration.
